title: Taraxacum melittostylum
intro: Taraxacum melittostylum, also known as the European dandelion, is a perennial plant of the dandelion genus, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and jagged-edged leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and improving liver and kidney function, making it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues and detoxification. The bioactive constituents of Taraxacum melittostylum include flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and triterpene saponins, which contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Taraxacum melittostylum is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to support liver and kidney health, and to alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders.
taxonomy: Taraxacum melittostylum is classified within the family Asteraceae, which includes other daisy-like flowering plants. It is a type of dandelion, specifically within the Taraxacum genus. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and physical appearance. Taraxacum melittostylum is further grouped under the tribe Cichorieae, within the subfamily Cichorioideae. The exact species classification of Taraxacum melittostylum may vary depending on the source and taxonomy.
